title: State Expansion and Economic Imperialism
description: This exercise will focus on an introduction to imperialism using the themes of CDI and GOV and addressing learning objectives A and B. It will have 2 types based on the two LOs and themes, scaled x6 (total 12).

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows. 
**

>Dear Minister, I have never ceased to draw the attention of my compatriots on the need to turn our view towards overseas territories. History teaches us that countries with small territories have a moral and material interest in extending their influence beyond their narrow borders. Greece founded opulent cities, home to arts and civilization, on the shores of the Mediterranean. Later, Venice built its grandeur on its maritime and commercial relations no less than on its political success. The Netherlands have thirty million subjects in the Indies who trade tropical products for the products of the mother country. It is in this service to the cause of humanity and progress that subordinated peoples appear as useful members to the great family of nations. More than any other, a trading and manufacturing nation like ours must do its best to secure opportunities for its workers, whether intellectual, capitalist, or manual. These patriotic concerns have dominated my life. They are what caused the creation of the African effort.

-From a letter to the Prime Minister of Belgium from King Leopold II, 1890. 

**Which of the following illustrates the broad historical context for the letter above? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: Citation: Rutz, Michael A. King Leopold’s Congo and the “Scramble for Africa”: A Short History with Documents. Indianapolis: Hackett Publishing Company, Inc., 2018. http://pi.lib.uchicago.edu/1001/cat/bib/11454195.

choices.content: Industrial capitalism meant high demand for raw materials.
Imperialism meant increased respect for the sovereignty of states and people groups.
Globalization increased the demand for cheaper and more efficient transportation of goods.
Nationalism decreased European competition and military investment.

**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.
**

>Dear Minister, I have never ceased to draw the attention of my compatriots on the need to turn our view towards overseas territories. History teaches us that countries with small territories have a moral and material interest in extending their influence beyond their narrow borders. Greece founded opulent cities, home to arts and civilization, on the shores of the Mediterranean. Later, Venice built its grandeur on its maritime and commercial relations no less than on its political success. The Netherlands have thirty million subjects in the Indies who trade tropical products for the products of the mother country. It is in this service to the cause of humanity and progress that subordinated peoples appear as useful members to the great family of nations. More than any other, a trading and manufacturing nation like ours must do its best to secure opportunities for its workers, whether intellectual, capitalist, or manual. These patriotic concerns have dominated my life. They are what caused the creation of the African effort.

-From a letter to the Prime Minister of Belgium from King Leopold II, 1890. 

**Based on the excerpt above and your knowledge of world history, the author is motivated by which of the following cultural developments? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: Citation: Rutz, Michael A. King Leopold’s Congo and the “Scramble for Africa”: A Short History with Documents. Indianapolis: Hackett Publishing Company, Inc., 2018. http://pi.lib.uchicago.edu/1001/cat/bib/11454195.

**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows. 
**

>Dear Minister, I have never ceased to draw the attention of my compatriots on the need to turn our view towards overseas territories. History teaches us that countries with small territories have a moral and material interest in extending their influence beyond their narrow borders. Greece founded opulent cities, home to arts and civilization, on the shores of the Mediterranean. Later, Venice built its grandeur on its maritime and commercial relations no less than on its political success. The Netherlands have thirty million subjects in the Indies who trade tropical products for the products of the mother country. It is in this service to the cause of humanity and progress that subordinated peoples appear as useful members to the great family of nations. These patriotic concerns have dominated my life. They are what caused the creation of the African effort.

**The sentiments expressed in the letter best illustrate which of the following developments from 1750 to 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

[[☃ explanation 1]]

options.explanation: Citation: Rutz, Michael A. King Leopold’s Congo and the “Scramble for Africa”: A Short History with Documents. Indianapolis: Hackett Publishing Company, Inc., 2018. http://pi.lib.uchicago.edu/1001/cat/bib/11454195.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Between 1800 and 1900, industrial empires often had better military technology and a stronger navy than other states. Which of the following statements best illustrates the effect of these advantages on industrial empires during this period? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: better trade deals and access to local markets
higher tariffs and shifts toward national economies
regional wars over access to factories and manufacturing centers
widespread adoption of Christian beliefs and practices

assessment_items.question_content:
**Using indirect rule, imperial powers established schools in colonies that taught European culture and language. Which of the following illustrates an indirect cultural effect of these education systems on children in colonies between 1750 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They were taught that they were inferior, less civilized, and needed knowledge from the imperial power.
They learned aspects of their traditions, language, and belief systems that were previously forgotten.
They learned technical skills that were valued in industrial capitalism that allowed them access to new labor markets.
They were taught the history of imperialism through a critical lens and were able to disrupt industrial exploitation.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best represents the types of technological advancements that enabled imperialism from 1750 to 1900?  
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following best describes the likely intent of the painting above?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A battle between the Chinese and Japanese armies. The Japanese rising sun flag waves high in the top corner.
options.caption: Source: This is a color woodblock print from 1894 by the artist Mizuno Toshikata, called, “Long Live the Great Japanese Empire! A Great Victory for Our Troops in the Assault on Songhwan.” Public domain.

choices.content: to gain support for imperialist projects
to educate recruits on military strategy
to build a case for racial superiority
to describe the events of the battle in exact detail

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the image below to answer the question that follows.**

[[☃ image 1]]

**A historian would most likely use this painting to illustrate which of the following developments in 1750 to 1900 CE?
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A battle between the Chinese and Japanese armies. The Japanese rising sun flag waves high in the top corner.
options.caption: Source: This is a color woodblock print from 1894 by the artist Mizuno Toshikata, called, “Long Live the Great Japanese Empire! A Great Victory for Our Troops in the Assault on Songhwan.” Public domain.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows
**

>MR. PRESIDENT, the times call for candor. The Philippines are ours forever, “territory belonging to the United States,” as the Constitution calls them. And just beyond the Philippines are China’s illimitable markets. We will not retreat from either. We will not repudiate our duty in the archipelago. We will not abandon our opportunity in the Orient. We will not renounce our part in the mission of our race, trustee, under God, of the civilization of the world. And we will move forward to our work, not howling out regrets like slaves whipped to their burdens but with gratitude for a task worthy of our strength and thanksgiving to Almighty God that He has marked us as His chosen people, henceforth to lead in the regeneration of the world.

From *In Support of an American Empire* by Albert Beveridge, Congressional Record of the 56th Congress, 1900.  

**Ideas such as those expressed in the excerpt above would have been most readily endorsed by those in which of the following groups?  
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Japanese samurai of the *sonnō jōi* movement

assessment_items.question_content:
**Use the excerpt below to answer the question that follows.
**

>MR. PRESIDENT, the times call for candor. The Philippines are ours forever, “territory belonging to the United States,” as the Constitution calls them. And just beyond the Philippines are China’s illimitable markets. We will not retreat from either. We will not repudiate our duty in the archipelago. We will not abandon our opportunity in the Orient. We will not renounce our part in the mission of our race, trustee, under God, of the civilization of the world. And we will move forward to our work, not howling out regrets like slaves whipped to their burdens but with gratitude for a task worthy of our strength and thanksgiving to Almighty God that He has marked us as His chosen people, henceforth to lead in the regeneration of the world.

From "In Support of an American Empire" by Albert Beveridge, Congressional Record of the 56th Congress, 1900.  

**Which of the following factors could a historian reasonably infer motivated Beveridge’s desire to expand the American empire to China? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Many forms of imperialism were practiced throughout the world from 1750 to 1900 CE. Which of the following represents the idea that an imperial power could gain control over a community without claiming sovereignty or establishing a colony? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**In 1908, under the pressure of the Congo Reform Association and other international groups, the Belgian Government took control of the Congo Free State from King Leopold II and renamed it the Belgian Congo. This process in Congo is most similar to which of the following processes between 1750 and 1900 CE? 
**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Dutch government control in Southeast Asia
United States government control of the Philippines
